name: Brax (JAX-Accelerated Physics Simulation for RL)

performance.notes: JAX JIT + GPU/TPU; millions of environments batched in parallel; 100-1000x faster than MuJoCo for parallel RL

agent_readiness.notes: Google's JAX-based differentiable physics for massively parallel RL. import brax. from brax import envs. env = envs.get_environment('ant'). state = env.reset(rng=jax.random.PRNGKey(0)). state = jax.vmap(env.reset)(jax.random.split(rng, 4096)). Massive parallelism: 4096 envs simultaneously. Differentiable: gradients through physics. Environments: ant, hopper, humanoid, half-cheetah, walker2d, pusher. Gymnasium wrapper: brax.envs.wrappers.gym.VectorGymWrapper. JAX required. For ablation and large-scale HPO much faster than MuJoCo.

gotchas: JAX required — different programming model from PyTorch
Physics approximations differ from MuJoCo — results may not transfer
Less stable than MuJoCo for complex contact-rich tasks
Steep JAX learning curve (functional, no in-place operations)
Documentation is thin compared to MuJoCo
best_when: Massively parallel RL training (thousands of environments simultaneously)
When GPU/TPU is the accelerator and JAX is the framework
Differentiable physics for gradient-based policy optimization
summary: Brax is Google's JAX physics simulator for massively parallel RL. jax.vmap(env.reset)(jax.random.split(rng, 4096)) runs 4096 envs simultaneously on GPU/TPU. Differentiable physics for gradient-based optimization. Ant, hopper, humanoid, humanoidstandup environments. Apache-2.0. 100-1000x faster than MuJoCo for parallel RL. JAX required. Less physically accurate than MuJoCo. Best for large-scale RL HPO where speed matters more than physics accuracy.
